description:
You are a security-oriented log analyst.  
    Before you can start correlating SSH login patterns, you need an
    analyst-specific key-pair and a tiny factsheet about that key.  
    Create it from the terminal and leave an auditable trail that an automated
    checker can parse.

    1.  Make sure the directory /home/user/.ssh exists; if it does not, create
        it with permissions 700.

    2.  Generate a brand-new RSA key pair **without** a passphrase and store
        it under the exact base‐name “log_analyst_rsa” inside /home/user/.ssh
        (i.e. the private key must become  
        /home/user/.ssh/log_analyst_rsa and the public key must become  
        /home/user/.ssh/log_analyst_rsa.pub).

        • Key type: RSA  
        • Key size: 2048 bits  
        • Comment field: leave it empty

    3.  Extract the SHA256 fingerprint of the **public** key you just created.

    4.  Create one single-line log file called
        /home/user/analysis/ssh_key_generation.log that looks **exactly** like
        this (three fields separated by a single TAB character):

        PRIVATE_KEY_PATH<TAB>PUBLIC_KEY_PATH<TAB>FINGERPRINT

        Concretely, after successful execution the file must literally contain
        one line with values in this order:

        /home/user/.ssh/log_analyst_rsa<TAB>/home/user/.ssh/log_analyst_rsa.pub<TAB>SHA256:████

        Replace “████” with the real fingerprint string reported by
        “ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/.ssh/log_analyst_rsa.pub”.  
        There must be **no** extra spaces, no surrounding quotes and no extra
        newlines.

    5.  Permissions must be:

        • /home/user/.ssh/log_analyst_rsa          → 600  
        • /home/user/.ssh/log_analyst_rsa.pub      → 644  
        • /home/user/analysis/ssh_key_generation.log → 644

    When finished, the automated grader will:

        a. Check that both key files exist at the exact paths above.  
        b. Confirm their permissions.  
        c. Compute the SHA256 fingerprint of the public key and verify that the
           log file’s third field matches it byte-for-byte.  
        d. Ensure the log file is only one line long and uses TAB separators.

    Hint: this entire task can be accomplished with roughly
    two or three standard ssh-keygen and mkdir/chmod commands.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth information for the automated checker.            -->
    <!-- These details MUST NOT be revealed in the public task.         -->

    expected_private_key_path:  /home/user/.ssh/log_analyst_rsa
    expected_public_key_path:   /home/user/.ssh/log_analyst_rsa.pub
    expected_log_path:          /home/user/analysis/ssh_key_generation.log

    log_line_format_regex: ^/home/user/\.ssh/log_analyst_rsa\t/home/user/\.ssh/log_analyst_rsa\.pub\tSHA256:[A-Za-z0-9+/=]+$

    file_permission_checks:
        /home/user/.ssh/log_analyst_rsa      600
        /home/user/.ssh/log_analyst_rsa.pub  644
        /home/user/analysis/ssh_key_generation.log 644
name: task_000000_1ae16768
